title: Mouse Perfusion
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"840u0","text":"This protocol is used to perfuse mice and isolate their brain for cryosectioning.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Sedate mice with 120mg/kg Euthanyl then open their chest cavity to expose their heart.
Insert the needle tip into the left ventricle and cut the right atria. Begin slowly injecting 10mL 1X phosphate buffered saline (PBS) over 5 minutes.
Once all of the 1X PBS has been perfused, begin injecting 10mL 4% paraformaldehyde or 10% formalin over 5 minutes.
Extract brain and incubate them in either 4% paraformaldehyde or 10% formalin for 72 hours at 4 o C.
Incubate brain in 10% sucrose for 24 hours at 4 o C.
Incubate brain in 20% sucrose for 24 hours at 4 o C.
Incubate brain in 30% sucrose for 24 hours at 4 o C.
Decant brain from sucrose solution and flash freeze in isopentane at -35 to -45 o C for 1 minute.
creator: Haley Geertsma
